steps.title: Identify the core crisis and its solution
steps.content: The passage identifies the crisis root: coins below 80% silver were unsuitable for trade, depleting confidence. To relieve this crisis, Abd'astart I must restore coins to above 80% silver.

steps.title: Match the solution to the choices
steps.content: Choice A achieves this: keeping each coin's absolute silver amount (as in 367 BCE coins) while decreasing weight increases the silver percentage above 80%, making coins suitable for trade again. Choices B, C, and D fail to restore coins above 80%—B proclaims a future standard without solving current unsuitability; D maintains the problematic 74.2% proportion; C adds copper, worsening debasement. This matches choice A — A is correct.
